Site.name: HELM Studio
Site.tagline: Bespoke AI automations for European creators.

Hero.title: We build [bespoke] [AI] automations for content creators.
Hero.footnote: You pay only if it actually saved you time.

HowItWorks.title: The [two-week] pilot.
HowItWorks.intro: No abstract quotes. No signed commitment. We build an automation stitched to your workflow, you use it free, you pay only if it actually saved you time on a real video.
HowItWorks.step01Title: A call with a technical lead
HowItWorks.step01Body: We figure out together which recurring task drains you most: setup, research, repurposing, descriptions, sponsor matching… We pick the starting point with you, not for you.
HowItWorks.step02Body: Our technical team prepares the automation tailored to your specific flow — not a generic template, a solution stitched to the channel you actually run.
HowItWorks.step03Body: You try the automation on a real video, end to end. At the end of the week, you decide: if it saved you time, you pay a flat fee to keep it. Otherwise, no hard feelings.

WhatWeBuild.title: Not a catalogue. A [sample book].
WhatWeBuild.intro: Every pilot starts from something concrete. Tap an automation below to see how we think about it — not generic templates, solutions stitched to your specific workflow.

items.label: Transcript → Notion
items.problem: Your video transcripts are searchable assets for the future, but copying them into Notion by hand is something you never actually do.
items.solution: Webhook on new YouTube upload → transcript extraction (auto or human-edited if available) → automated creation of a Notion page with sections, highlighted quotes, and niche tags.
items.example: From a 25-minute video to a Notion page with 8 chapters and 4 searchable quotes in under 60 seconds.

items.label: B-roll search
items.problem: You're looking for a specific shot from your old videos and end up scrubbing for 20 minutes without finding it.
items.solution: We index your entire library (frame + audio) in a vector database. You search by voice: "when I soldered the red PCB" → exact timestamps returned.
items.example: 280 videos indexed. Find a precise scene in 3 seconds instead of 20 minutes of scrubbing.

items.label: Thumbnail A/B
items.problem: The wrong thumbnail costs views, but you have no time to test 5 variants on every upload.
items.solution: We generate 5–10 variants in your style (text, crop, subject), test them via the YouTube experiments API, and report only the winner with numbers.
items.example: 7 thumbnails tested in the 48h after upload. Keep it or swap it with one click.

items.label: Sponsor matching
items.problem: The right brands for your channel rarely reach out. The wrong ones email you every week.
items.solution: RAG on your channel + audience analysis → a dynamic media kit. We identify brands whose products match your content and prepare the pitch email in your tone.
items.example: 6 brands identified in the retro-tech niche, 3 positive replies in 2 weeks.

items.label: Description writer
items.problem: Writing description + chapters + tags + affiliate links on every video is 20 repetitive minutes you avoid.
items.solution: Pipeline that takes transcript + metadata and generates SEO description in your tone, chapters with timestamps, optimised YouTube tags, and a filled affiliate block.
items.example: From raw video to publishable description in 90 seconds instead of 20 minutes.

items.label: Chapter generator
items.problem: Chapters lift retention but adding them by hand is the first thing you skip when you're in a hurry.
items.solution: LLM analyses the video structure from transcript and audio, proposes chapters with titles in your tone and exact timestamps. You approve or tweak.
items.example: 12 chapters generated in 30s on a 25-minute video, titles written like you'd write them.

items.label: Title testing
items.problem: You have 3 titles in mind, you don't know which one performs best, and you don't want to be a YouTube growth guy.
items.solution: We generate 5 variants in your style (zero clickbait), test via YouTube experiments, report the best performer with data.
items.example: 5 titles on a video, the side-pick you wouldn't have chosen wins: +22% CTR vs original.
items.timeSaved: Measurable CTR uplift, ~10 min per upload

items.label: Shorts editor
items.problem: Cutting 3 Shorts from each long-form is hours of editing that often gets skipped entirely.
items.solution: LLM finds the most clip-worthy moments using retention curve + transcript, generates the cut, zoom, and captions. You approve the top 3.
items.example: 8 Shorts ready from a 25-minute long-form. Publish or discard in a few minutes.

items.label: Setup VM legacy
items.problem: Every time you bring back a vintage card you lose a whole night installing the right OS and drivers.
items.solution: Pre-configured VM/imaging library (Win 98 SE, XP, driver variants). Single command to spin up a test environment.
items.example: VMAS test ready in 90 seconds, no more Ctrl+A spammed until 4am.

items.label: Outlier research
items.problem: Essay-style videos take hours of research across academic PDFs, Substack newsletters, OECD reports that you cite by hand each time.
items.solution: Custom RAG that ingests your reading list + source PDFs, generates structured summaries and thematic clusters. Every fact cited with source.
items.example: 22 sources aggregated into a 4-page brief with a timeline and thematic clusters.

items.label: Newsletter draft
items.problem: The newsletter that follows the video is 90 minutes of rewriting that drains your post-upload energy.
items.solution: Transcript → Substack draft in your tone, with sections, lead, and link to the video. You review for 15 minutes and publish.
items.example: A 1200-word newsletter ready in 4 minutes, with a quarter-hour of polish on top.

items.label: Podcast show notes
items.problem: Your podcast show notes are SEO and audience growth, but you keep pushing them off because of fatigue.
items.solution: From podcast audio → full show notes: 3 paragraphs, timestamps, key quotes, guest bio, social links.
items.example: 35 minutes of podcast → 6 chapters + 8 quotes + guest bio in 90 seconds.

items.label: Comment triage
items.problem: Under your videos you get 500 comments, 90% noise, 10% important questions you'd love to see but don't.
items.solution: We classify the comments (question, idea, fan, hater, sponsor) and send a daily digest of just the "tier 1" ones to reply to.
items.example: From 320 comments on a video, 28 flagged "reply". Answered all of them in 12 minutes.

items.label: Affiliate aggregator
items.problem: You keep 15 affiliate links in mind + an old spreadsheet + copy-paste descriptions video after video.
items.solution: Affiliate database with automatic lookup → links injected for the products mentioned in the video (transcript-based).
items.example: 14 links automatically inserted into the description of an audio teardown, with disclosure.

items.label: Reading vlog highlights
items.problem: Reading vlogs have 30 minutes of daily life and 5 strong lines about books scattered everywhere. Social audiences skip them.
items.solution: We extract the "best of" book moments from your vlogs → reel/short version with optimised captions.
items.example: 10 highlights like "why Colette is working on me" extracted from 4 vlogs.

items.label: Sponsor brief
items.problem: When a brand reaches out you need to reply with an updated media kit. The one you have is from 2024.
items.solution: Dynamic media kit (audience demo, top 10 videos, average CPM, examples of sponsor integrations) always up to date. Just forward it.
items.example: Brand asks for pricing → you send an updated PDF in 30 seconds instead of spending 1h refreshing the stats.

About.title: What [Morningside] is for companies. For creators.
About.origin: HELM Studio was founded in 2026 as the European spin-off of Morningside AI, the first agency to formalise the "build custom AI workflows" model — solutions stitched to the client, not generic templates recycled from one channel to the next.
About.differentiation: Morningside works with companies. We do the same work for people who run a channel, a podcast, a newsletter — individuals, not departments.
About.people: When we write to you, there isn't a sales rep behind the message — it's the technical team that builds the work itself. No resellers, no middle layer: you talk directly with the person whose hands are on the code.
About.manifesto: Real automation shows up on your publishing calendar, not on a sales deck.

Footer.manifesto: We build tools that earn time, not clicks.
